title: Native CatBoost categorical-diversity blend
summary: The public competition write-up used CatBoost but dropped Driver and one-hot encoded objects, while CatBoost documentation recommends native categorical handling and ordered target statistics to reduce target leakage.
rationale: The public competition write-up used CatBoost but dropped Driver and one-hot encoded objects, while CatBoost documentation recommends native categorical handling and ordered target statistics to reduce target leakage. The current best pipeline uses frequencies/categories, but a dedicated CatBoost model with categorical combinations can add ensemble diversity.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoostClassifier on minimally encoded data with Driver, Race, Compound, Race_Year, Driver_Race, Race_Compound, Driver_Compound, and binned numerical interactions as categorical/string features. Use ordered boosting, AUC eval, class weights or scale_pos_weight only if CV helps, then blend OOF-calibrated ranks with the existing AutoGluon/GBM predictions.
expected_effect: May capture high-cardinality driver/race strategy tendencies that frequency features miss, while providing a different error profile for a weighted rank blend.
risk: Native CTRs can still overfit if validation is row-random or if categorical combinations are too granular. Blend by OOF AUC, not public score chasing.
